sentences.text: In the absence of an institution’s selection criteria, Aetna considers kidney transplantation medically necessary when all of the following criteria below are met: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Member has completed an evaluation and been accepted by the kidney transplant committee at the kidney transplantation center.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Absence of malignancy (except for non-melanomatous skin cancers or low-grade prostate cancer) or the malignancy has had curative therapy (e.g., surgical resection of non- invasive squamous cell or basal cell skin cancer) or the estimated risk of recurrence of the malignancy is less than 10% within the next 2 years.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Absence of symptomatic HIV infection, as defined by all of the following: a. CD4 count greater than 200 cells/mm 3 for more than 6 months; and b. HIV-1 RNA (viral load) undetectable; and c. On stable anti-viral therapy for more than 3 months; and d. No other complications from AIDS, such as opportunistic infection (e.g., aspergillus, coccidiomycosis, resistant fungal infections, tuberculosis), Kaposi's sarcoma or other neoplasms; and 6.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Attending physician determines that there is no prohibitive cardiovascular risk; and 7.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Severity of disease: a. Member is already on hemodialysis or continuous ambulatory peritoneal dialysis (CAPD); or b. Member has chronic renal failure with anticipated deterioration to end stage renal disease, where member is seeking precertification for cadaveric kidney transplantationFootnote1* or c. Member has end stage renal disease, evidenced by a creatinine clearance below 20 ml/min or development of symptoms of uremia, and member is seeking precertification for a living donor kidney transplantation; and Footnote1* Note: Given waiting periods for cadaveric donors averaging 1 to 4 years, kidney transplantation is considered medically necessary for persons with severe chronic renal failure with anticipated progression to end stage renal disease.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Severe chronic renal failure is defined as a creatinine clearance of less than 30 ml/min; Kidney transplant is not considered medically necessary for persons who do not meet the transplanting institution's protocol selection criteria, or in the absence of a protocol, for persons who have any of the following (not an all-inclusive list): 1. Active vasculitis; or 2.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: For persons undergoing kidney transplantation due to diabetic nephropathy, a combined kidney/pancreas transplantation may be considered medically necessary under some circumstances (see CPB 0587 - Pancreas Kidney Transplantation). 2.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: C. Renal Auto-Transplantation and Ex-Vivo Bench Surgery Aetna considers auto-transplantation and ex-vivo repair medically necessary where repair of the kidney, ureter, renal artery or its branches are not amenable to in-situ reconstruction.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: D. Belatacept (Nulojix) Aetna considers the use of belatacept (Nulojix) medically necessary for the prevention of acute rejection in kidney transplant recipients who are sero-positive for the Epstein Barr virus (EBV).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Aetna considers belatacept experimental, investigational, or unproven for the prophylaxis of organ rejection in other transplanted organs because its effectiveness for the prevention of acute rejection in organ transplant other than kidney has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: E. Equine Anti-Thymocyte Immune Globulin Aetna considers equine anti-thymocyte immune globulin (Atgam) medically necessary for the following indications: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Aetna considers equine anti-thymocyte immune globulin experimental, investigational, or unproven for all other indications.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: F. Renal Auto-Transplantation Aetna considers renal auto-transplantation medically necessary for the treatment of individuals with loin pain hematuria syndrome who have failed non-surgical therapies including analgesics. II.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven Aetna considers the following experimental, investigational, or unproven: A. Gene Microarrays for Diagnosis of Rejection Aetna considers the use of gene microarrays (e.g., the Kidney Microscope Diagnostic System (MMDx-Kidney)) in diagnosis of rejection of kidney transplantation experimental, investigational, or unproven because of insufficient evidence of their effectiveness.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: B. Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven Markers of Acute Rejection Aetna considers measurement of cytokines (e.g., cytokine-14, interleukin-1 beta (IL-1β), IL-2, IL-4, IL-6, granulocyte-macrophage colony-stimulating factor (GM-CSF), monocyte chemoattractant protein-1 (MCP-1), and tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF-α); not an all- inclusive list) for the diagnosis of acute renal allograft rejection experimental, investigational, or unproven because the effectiveness of this approach has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: C. Experimental, Investigational, or Unproven Markers of Rejection Risk Aetna considers the following experimental, investigational, or unproven because the effectiveness of this approach has not been established: 1.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: D. Bisphosphonates Aetna considers bisphosphonates experimental, investigational, or unproven for the treatment of low bone mineral density after kidney transplantation because their effectiveness of this indication has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: E. Pre-Conditioning Therapy Aetna considers pre-conditioning therapy (e.g., immune-adsorption or rituximab) in ABO- incompatible kidney transplantation experimental, investigational, or unproven because the effectiveness of this approach has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: F. Biomarkers of Acute Kidney Injury Aetna considers urinary neutrophil gelatinase-associated lipocalin (NGAL) and liver-type fatty acid-binding protein (L-FABP) experimental, investigational, or unproven as biomarkers of acute kidney injury following kidney transplantation because the effectiveness of this approach has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: G. FASL mRNA Aetna considers Fas ligand (FASL) mRNA detection experimental, investigational, or unproven as a diagnostic marker for acute renal rejection because the effectiveness of this approach has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: H. Urinary Biomarkers (e.g., Chemokines, Extracellular Vesicles, and Monocyte Chemoattractant Protein-1) Aetna considers urinary biomarkers (e.g., chemokines including CXCL9 and CXCL10 (e.g., One Lambda CXCL10 urine test), alone or in combination; extracellular vesicles; monocyte chemoattractant protein-1 (MCP-1/CCL2) experimental, investigational, or unproven for detection and monitoring of renal graft rejection because the effectiveness of this approach has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: I. Donor-Derived Cell-Free DNA Testing Aetna considers donor-derived cell-free DNA testing (e.g., Allosure, Prospera Kidney, VitaGraft Kidney Baseline + 1st Plasma Test, VitaGraft Kidney Subsequent, and VitaGraft Kidney 2.0) experimental, investigational, or unproven for consideration of tapering of immunosuppression therapy in kidney transplant recipients, and monitoring acute rejection following renal transplantation because the effectiveness of these approaches has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: J. Measurement of Angiotensin II Type 1 (AT1) Receptors or AT1 Antibodies for Evaluation of Renal Transplantation Candidates / Recipients Aetna considers measurement of angiotensin II type 1 (AT1) receptors or AT1 antibodies experimental, investigational, or unproven for evaluation of renal transplantation candidates / recipients because the effectiveness of this approach has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: K. Complement Inhibitors (e.g., Eculizumab) for the Treatment of Antibody-Mediated Rejection in Renal Transplantation Recipients Aetna considers complement inhibitors (e.g., eculizumab) experimental, investigational, or unproven for the treatment of antibody-mediated rejection in renal transplantation recipients because their effectiveness for this indication has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: L. Belimumab for the Treatment of Antibody-Mediated Rejection in Renal Transplantation Recipients Aetna considers belimumab experimental, investigational, or unproven for the treatment of antibody-mediated rejection in renal transplantation recipients because its effectiveness for this indication has not been established. See CPB 0818 - Belimumab (Benlysta).
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: M. Genotyping Donors and Recipients Before Renal Transplantation Aetna considers genotyping donors and recipients before renal transplantation experimental, investigational, or unproven because its effectiveness for this indication has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: N. Recombinant Human Erythropoietin Aetna considers recombinant human erythropoietin (e.g., Epogen, Procrit, and Retacrit) experimental, investigational, or unproven for nephron-protection in persons undergoing kidney transplantation because its effectiveness for this indication has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: O. TruGraf Blood Gene Expression Test Aetna considers the TruGraf blood gene expression test (TruGraf Kidney (Eurofins Transplant Genomics, Inc.)) experimental, investigational, or unproven for the management of kidney transplant recipients because its effectiveness has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: P. Plasminogen Activator Evaluation Aetna considers plasminogen activator evaluation experimental, investigational, or unproven as part of a hypercoagulable workup prior to kidney transplantation because the effectiveness of this approach has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Q. Evaluation of Urine Immunocytology Aetna considers evaluation of urine immunocytology for T cells experimental, investigational, or unproven for the diagnosis of acute kidney rejection because its role has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: S. DNA Methylation Aetna considers the use of DNA methylation experimental, investigational, or unproven as a biomarker of post-transplant complications in kidney transplantation because its clinical value has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: T. Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning Method for Predicting Graft Survival Aetna considers artificial intelligence/machine learning method experimental, investigational, or unproven for predicting graft survival in kidney transplantation because the effectiveness of this approach has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: U. Panniculectomy in Kidney Transplant Candidates Aetna considers panniculectomy experimental, investigational, or unproven if used to improve kidney transplant outcomes or improve kidney transplant candidacy because the effectiveness of this approach has not been established.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: V. myOLARIS-KTdx Aetna considers myOLARIS-KTdx experimental, investigational, or unproven for diagnosis and prognosis of kidney allograft rejection because the effectiveness of this approach has not been established. III.
sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.label: CLAIM

sentences.text: Ebeling (2007) noted that organ transplant candidates should be assessed and pre-transplantation bone disease should be treated.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that treatment with bisphosphonates, vitamin D sterol or calcitonin after kidney transplantation may protect against immunosuppression-induced reductions in BMD and prevent fracture.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that despite promising results in diagnosis of histopathological lesions using microarrays, the low dynamic range of microarrays and large measured expression changes within the probes for the same gene continue to cast doubts on their readiness for diagnosis of rejection.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that although tubulitis is a hallmark of acute T cell-mediated rejection, detection of T cells in urine sediment was insufficiently sensitive and insufficiently specific for diagnosing acute rejection in this cohort of kidney transplant recipients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the results of the meta-analysis showed that the accuracy of pre-transplantation sCD30 for predicting post-transplantation AR was poor.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Lv and colleagues (2012) noted that results from published studies on the association of donor or recipient interleukin (IL)-6 -174G/C (rs1800795) polymorphism with AR of renal allograft are conflicting.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that donor high producer genotype (G/G and G/C) of IL-6 -174G/C polymorphism had a tendency of decreased risk for AR, although it was not statistically significant.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that longitudinal monitoring of dd-cfDNA may be useful as a dynamic biomarker to examine the health of the kidney allograft after rejection treatment.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Bu et al (2021) noted that the use of routine monitoring of dd-cfDNA after kidney transplant may allow clinicians to identify subclinical allograft injury and intervene before development of clinically evident graft injury.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors stated that further investigation is needed to establish the optimal interval of monitoring as there is clear multi-factorial value considering dd-cfDNA as part of the clinical assessment of the patient.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: dd-cfDNA showed better performance than creatinine in detecting T-cell mediated rejection (TCMR), especially at 15 days post-transplant.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The study found that, while dd-cfDNA shows promise, especially in early detection and in specific rejection types like TCMR, its overall diagnostic accuracy in this study was limited.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 2025) explored the clinical utility of donor-derived cell-free DNA (dd-cfDNA) testing in pediatric kidney transplant recipients, focusing on its role in identifying biopsy-proven acute rejection (BPAR) and immune activation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Results showed that patients with dd- cfDNA levels >1% were significantly more likely to have BPAR (59%) and dnHLAab (88.1%), particularly class II antibodies (DQ and DR) and donor-specific antibodies (DSAs).
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: 2025) investigated whether combining two dd-cfDNA metrics -- fraction (%) and absolute quantity (copies/mL) -- could improve the accuracy of diagnosing kidney transplant rejection.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Pinelli and colleagues (2017) noted that endothelial cell antigens have been reported as potential targets for antibodies in the context of organ transplantation, leading to increased risk for graft failure.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Graft survival was monitored for 5 years post-transplant, and secondary outcomes, including biopsy-proven rejection, proteinuria, biopsy-proven vasculopathy, and renal function based on serum creatinine were also assessed for the first 2 to 3 years.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that while pre-existing and de-novo donor-specific HLA antibodies were associated with graft failure and many secondary outcomes, no statistical association was found for either anti-endothelial or anti- AT1R antibodies, indicating that these tests may not be the best predictors of graft outcome in living donor renal transplantation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Kang and colleagues (2022) noted that anti-AT1R antibodies (AT1R-Abs) have been recognized as non-HLA antibodies associated with allograft rejection and poor allograft outcomes following KTx.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the findings of this study showed that the presence of anti-AT1R-Abs was associated with a significantly higher risk of AMR and graft loss in KTx.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Complement Inhibitors (e.g., Eculizumab) for the Treatment of Antibody-Mediated Rejection Wan and colleagues (2018) stated that current treatments for AMR in kidney transplantation are based on low-quality data from a small number of controlled trials.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that newer studies evaluating rituximab showed little or no difference to early graft survival, and the efficacy of bortezomib and complement inhibitors for the treatment of AMR remains unclear.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: Belimumab for the Treatment of Antibody-Mediated Rejection Banham and colleagues (2018) stated that B cells produce allo-antibodies and activate allo-reactive T cells, negatively affecting kidney transplant survival.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that belimumab might be a useful adjunct to standard-of-care immunosuppression in renal transplantation, with no major increased risk of infection and potential beneficial effects on humoral allo-immunity.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that several polymorphisms in either the donor or the recipient or both have been associated with DGF in KTRs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that in the present era of extravesical uretero-vesical anastomosis, routine stenting has a limited role in decreasing MUC and may be associated with higher incidence of UTIs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that real time central molecular assessment was feasible and offered a useful new dimension in biopsy interpretation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that existing studies were limited in quality and have not yielded biomarkers for kidneys undergoing HMP that are able to predict post-transplant outcomes with sufficient accuracy to support routine clinical use.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that peri-operative, high-dose rHuEPO administration did not prevent DGF in deceased donor KTx.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that rHuEPO had a limited nephron-protective effect in patients undergoing KTx and did not increase the susceptibility to AEs.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that laparoscopic approach to RAT using a single extended iliac incision in the management of LPHS can be considered as a less invasive treatment compared to open RAT in selected patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that a MDT approach utilizing a RHB should be considered as a tool to select appropriate LPHS patients for RAT to achieve long-term success in reducing chronic pain and depression while increasing QOL.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that TruGraf v1 could potentially support a clinical decision enabling unnecessary surveillance biopsies with high confidence, making it an invaluable addition to the transplant physician's tool kit for managing patients.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that TruGraf is the 1st and only non- invasive test to be examined for clinical utility in determining rejection status of patients with stable renal function and showed promise of providing support for clinical decisions to avoid unnecessary surveillance biopsies with a high degree of confidence.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the findings of this study indicated the value of serial TruGraf testing in those transplant centers that do not perform surveillance biopsies as part of their standard of care.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that the immunocytochemical localization and evaluation of alpha sm-1, Thy-1, ATIII, and urokinase in kidney allografts may be useful adjuncts in the assessment of function in renal allografts.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that studies included in this review were heterogeneous in study design, biological samples, and outcome.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that miR-10b down-regulation mediates many aspects of disease pathogenicity of acute kidney allograft rejection.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that the up-regulation of miR-650 contributed to the development of acute renal allograft rejection by suppression of BCL11B, which led to apoptosis and inflammatory responses.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that miRNAs are emerging as important regulatory molecules of gene expression.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these findings suggested that miRNA expression patterns may serve as non-invasive diagnostic biomarkers to assess immune response and kidney allograft status.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that in addition to dn DSA screening, monitoring of CIITA and CTLA-4 in peripheral blood could offer useful information on the time course of the development of cAMR.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the strong association between ACR and allo-specific CD154+TcM may be useful in minimizing protocol biopsies among recipients at reduced rejection risk.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that alloreactive T-cell subsets, which express the inflammatory co- stimulator, CD154, showed wide-ranging susceptibility to CD28-costimulation blockade with belatacept.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that allo-specific T-cytotoxic memory cells distinguished primary RTR with quiescent allografts from those with dysfunction.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that this mini review provided updates and insights into the latest development of promising novel biomarkers for diagnosis and novel therapeutic interventions to prevent and treat CKTR.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that abdominal panniculectomy was feasible in patients suffering ESRD with no major post-operative complications; thus, converting previously ineligible patients into KTx candidates.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that these preliminary findings demonstrated that panniculectomy in ESRD patients appeared to be safe, although with a nominal increased risk for complications.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that urinary CXCL10 was associated with alloimmune and virus compartment-specific inflammation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that CXCL10 and metabolome analyzes were useful for evaluation of graft functions.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the identification of specific and sensitive biomarkers could potentially reduce unnecessary biopsies, resulting in more individualized treatment plans and improved health outcomes.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that the use of an integrated model of urinary chemokines and clinical markers for non- invasive monitoring of rejection could enable a reduction in the number of biopsies.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that urinary CXCL10/Cr reliably identified kidney allograft inflammation.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that this POC study reported the use of dd-cfDNA to directly inform IST management in a cohort of KTx recipients who were candidates for IST reduction.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that although several possible biomarkers have been proposed to predict AMR, ineffectiveness of current urinary biomarkers in early diagnosing of AMR patients and in distinguishing AMR subjects from patients with stable kidney function casts doubts on their applicability in the clinical setting.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that putative immune or structural protein candidate biomarkers have been identified by means of proteomic techniques in multiple sample types including urine, serum and fluid used to perfuse donor kidneys.
sentences.label: PREMISE

sentences.text: The authors concluded that evaluating the protein composition related to KT results could result in identification of biomarkers that provide insights into the graft functionality.
sentences.label: PREMISE
